title: Live Speech-to-Speech Translation: A Systems Guide

pages.title: Live Translation Trades Wait For Risk
pages.kicker: The system must choose when enough source meaning is stable to begin speaking.

cards.title: Wait for context
cards.body: More source speech improves word order and ambiguity resolution.

cards.title: Speak early
cards.body: Lower delay keeps conversation natural but increases commitment risk.

cards.title: Revision policy
cards.body: Text can change quietly; spoken audio already played cannot.

pages.title: Translation Is Session-Controlled
pages.kicker: Terminology, speaker state, code-switching, and privacy should persist across the live session.

cards.title: Terminology
cards.body: Hotwords and named entities need scoped target-language forms.

cards.title: Speaker identity
cards.body: Voice preservation must remain consented and stable across turns.

cards.title: Code-switching
cards.body: Language routing should handle mixed speech without resetting context.

cards.title: Privacy boundary
cards.body: Visual and audio context expand what the session can expose.
